title: Contest — an affected party's signed counter-claim is surfaced
summary: Resolution complete — verified · with a standing contestation

steps.title: Resolve AgentName → AgentAddr
steps.plain: Look the agent up by its name and get back a small, signed “address card” saying where to find it.

steps.title: Verify AgentAddr — Ed25519 over JCS, resolver pinned
steps.detail: signature valid; signed by the pinned index resolver
steps.plain: Check the card was signed by the one directory key you already trust. A valid signature from the wrong key is still refused.

steps.title: Select AgentFacts source
steps.plain: Decide where to read the agent's details from — here, the provider's own site.

steps.title: Fetch AgentFacts bundle
steps.detail: GET from the provider host (untrusted; it signs nothing)
steps.plain: Download the agent's profile. This host only stores files — it cannot vouch for anything, so trust will come from the signed credentials inside.

steps.title: Verify provider credential — W3C VC (JWT)
steps.detail: issuer did:key:z6Mkqvoa2c… · trusted · bound to agent_did
steps.plain: Check the provider's signed claim (already in the downloaded bundle) — and that it is really about THIS agent, so a valid claim about someone else can't be swapped in.

steps.title: Verify auditor credential — independent issuer
steps.plain: Check a second, independent reviewer's signed claim (also in the bundle) about the same agent. Two independent signers must agree before the facts are trusted.

steps.title: Surface contestation — affected-party counter-claim
steps.detail: [accuracy-dispute] “Returned output dropped the dispute clause; accuracy SLA breached.” · standing verified
steps.plain: A party the agent acted on has filed a signed complaint — verified against a receipt the agent itself signed, so it can't be faked. The client surfaces it next to the issuers' claims, rather than silently dropping it.

steps.title: Authorisation (operator-side) — context, not run here
steps.detail: AuthZEN · Txn-Tokens-for-Agents · delegation layers — decide if the action MAY proceed
steps.plain: Separately, an operator decides whether this action is allowed at all. That layer is cited here for context — this demo does not run it.

steps.title: Act on the verified endpoint
steps.plain: Every check passed, so now it is safe to actually call the agent.
